title: feature — one post-design unit-of-work with an assigned worktree and agent sequence
description: A feature is the first-class primitive of tasks.yaml v2. It groups related tasks that share a worktree + branch + agent sequence. The orchestrator (task 035) executes each feature inside its own git worktree by invoking the declared agent_sequence[] in order. Features can skip[] agents that aren't needed for their scope. Parallelism across features is governed by feature.depends_on[]. Refactor-004 introduces this primitive; it does not exist in tasks.yaml v1.

id.description: Feature ID; kebab-case slug prefixed with 'feat-'. Stable across tasks.yaml regenerations so depends_on references survive.

worktree.description: Directory name under .claude/worktrees/{worktree}/ where git-agent checks out this feature's branch. Matches feature id by convention but kept as a separate field so alternate worktree naming is possible.

branch.description: Git branch name; forked from main by git-agent at checkout-feature time.

priority.description: P0 = critical path, P1 = important, P2 = nice-to-have, P3 = polish / deferrable.

depends_on.description: Feature IDs that must complete before this feature's worktree can be opened. Governs inter-feature parallelism. Default: [] (no dependencies → runs as early as concurrency permits).

skip.description: Surfaces the feature does NOT touch. If skip contains 'mobile', the mobile-frontend-builder is skipped even if listed in agent_sequence. Allows tasks.yaml schema to remain uniform while feature scope varies.

agent_sequence.description: Ordered list of agents that must run on this feature. Orchestrator invokes each in order, passing only the tasks[] entries whose agent field matches the current sequence step. After the final agent, git-agent implicitly runs close-feature. agent_sequence never includes git-agent explicitly — the worktree lifecycle is orchestrator-owned.

tasks.description: Concrete work units inside this feature. Each task has an 'agent' field that must match one of the entries in agent_sequence — otherwise the task never runs.

summary.description: Short human-readable description of what this feature delivers. Appears in plan manifests + orchestrator progress logs.

brief_reference.description: Reference into brief.md (e.g., 'brief.md §12 auth flow') or a flow ID from docs/analysis/{platform}/flows.md. Lets builders cross-check feature scope against the brief.

affects_files.description: bug-015 Phase 2: glob list of files this feature is expected to mutate. PM authors at task-graph emission time. Orchestrator uses overlap detection to serialize features that share files (auto-add depends_on). Conservative — when in doubt, list more globs.

id.description: Task ID; kebab-case. Unique within the feature.

agent.description: The agent responsible for this task. MUST be a member of the parent feature.agent_sequence — otherwise the task never runs (schema + orchestrator both enforce).

depends_on.description: Task IDs within the SAME feature that must complete before this task runs. Cross-feature deps are expressed at feature.depends_on level, not task level.

skills.description: Skill IDs the agent should load when executing this task (from .claude/skills/). Example: ['stripe-connect', 'zod-refinement'].

priority.description: Inherits from parent feature if omitted.

integration_ref.description: Pointer into architecture.yaml when the task implements a vendor integration. Example: 'architecture.yaml#apps.api.integrations.payments'.

status.description: Updated by orchestrator + executing agents. Initial value: pending.

estimated_screens.description: For frontend tasks only: approximate screen count. Used for budget projection.

screens.description: feat-012: exact `{platform}/{screenId}` set this task owns. Frontend-builder tasks populated by PM from flows.md + screens-manifest.json; backend / tester / reviewer / devops tasks MUST leave empty. Two features claiming the same entry → tasks.yaml warnings[] (PM does not auto-resolve). Builders scope their work to this list; they do NOT read docs/screens/{platform}/*.html as a wildcard.

notes.description: Free-form notes, test fixtures, or architecture cross-references. Agents read this for context.
